Petition To Determine Those Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-22-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: M. Armon Cooper, Esq., counsel for Barbara Connors, Objector (appearing by video conference); Deborah Fox, Esq., counsel for Debra Dolch, Administrator.

The Court notes that a plethora of ostensible settlement agreements have been filed with the Court, albeit in counterparts. Ms. Fox and Mr. Cooper confirm that the last filing with the Court is the final Settlement Agreement. Upon comment by the Court, Ms. Fox represents that the signature of Mr. Neary to the Agreement has been obtained and was filed October 2, 2020.

In light of the foregoing developments, the Court determines that pursuant to the Settlement Agreement, it is the Court's understanding that the Petition filed by Barbara Connors can be granted and the Petition filed by the Dellingers can be denied. Mr. Cooper and Ms. Fox confirm the same. The Court takes the matter under submission but with the intention to rule as stated.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Court Examiner: Helen Yune Trowbridge; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. Bailiff: Deputy K. Johnson. (D204)

Petition To Determine Those Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-24-2020 Continued To Sep-28-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Deborah Fox appearing by Zoom for Debra Dolch (Administrator). John Ruepppel appeared in person for Jacob Dellinger (Individually & as Guardian ad Litem for Jillian Dellinger and for Claire Kottwitz. Armon Cooper appeared in person for Barbara Conner.

The Court stated that it was in attendance at the hearing on January 8th in which Judge Wiss presided over, that it observed counsel's colliliquy that day, that it has looked at everything filed, and addressed each of the conditional disclaimers filed by Michael S. Dellinger and Richard M. Dellinger on January 18, 2019. The Court stated each counsel's respective position with regard to the disclaimers. Mr. Cooper addressed the receipts and stated that the brothers never complied with their own requirement. Mr. Cooper further argued that under the Probate Code that they cannot disclaim after receiving a benefit, addressed Probate Codes 282 & 285, and stated that Probate Code 282 cannot become applicable without Probate Code 285. The Court stated the attorney in the Murphy case also made a mistake, that a lot of people dont' understand what a disclaimer is, addressed the unconditional disclaimer on January 30th noting that there is inconsistency, and that it is not a disclaimer it is a mistake. Mr. Rueppel stated that there was no admissable evidence re: intent, that Mr. Cooper cannot testify under Probate Code 1022, and that affidavits or declarations are accepted in an uncontested matter. The Court agreed that there are evidence hurdles that it will need to get over and stated that it plans to adjudicate the legal meaning of the documents filed on January 18, 2020 "on their four corners", and that it would rule on whether they are disclaimers or not. Mr. Rueppel stated that there is no evidence that the brothers accepted any interest in the estate and that there is no evidence of payments, etc. Further argument by Mr. Rueppel. Brief discussions are held. No argument by Ms. Fox. Mr. Cooper and Mr. Rueppel present their respective final arguments. The Court stated that it believes that there was an acceptanceof interest on the "four corners of the documents", that it finds that they are not valid disclaimers under the Probate Code, and that it doesn't have enough evidence to find that they are assignments. Ms. Fox stated that the Dellinger brothers were represented at one time and that she could reach out to their attorney and that it would be stepping back in as intestate heirs until the Court makes a further decision. The Court stated that maybe the parties could get together and discuss a settlement. Brief discussions are held. Ms. Fox stated that she would contact the attorney for the Dellinger brothers, that they could try and reach some sort of settlement, or they could come up with a memorandum of points and authorities to present to the Court. Mr. Cooper stated that the Dellinger brothers had notice of this hearing and they did not do anything. The Court contined the Petition to Determine Those Entitled to Distribution of Estate and the Petition to Determine Distribution Rights to September 28, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will issue an order. Mr. Cooper stated that they may amend their petition.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Dorothy Darby of AtkinsonBaker, CSR #11066, Tel. 818.551.7300, Email: www.depo.com. Deputy: K. Kabasares.

Petition To Determine Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Jul-08-2020 Continued To Jul-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Those Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-08-2020 Continued To Jul-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Deborah Fox for Debra Dolch (Administrator).
Armon Cooper appeared by telephone for Barbara Conner.
John Rueppel appeared by telephone for Jacob Dellinger (Guardian Ad Litem).

The Court asks the Parties how they are going to proceed. Mr. Rueppel stated that he believes this can be decided, that there is a legal issue of the effect of some disclaimers that were signed by two of the Heirs and that it is clear that the Heirs signed the disclaimers. Ms. Fox stated that she had suggested a meet and confer, and mediation, but the Parties wanted to see if the Court would make a decision today without an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Cooper stated that the matter should be decided on the papers, that no one is asking for an evidentiary hearing, that there are no disputed facts and that everything the Court needs is before it. The Court noted that the Parties are arguing about the intent of the documents and stated that it is not sure this can be decided on a morning calendar without a full hearing for the Parties to argue the effect of those documents. Ms. Fox stated that she requested that the Former Administrator prepare an accounting for the period of August 24, 2018 through August 12, 2019, that her Client needs information to prepare the inventory and appraisal, and that they have requested bank statements. Mr. Cooper stated that there is some confusion and that his Client delivered all of the information that was available to her to Ms. Fox's Client. Ms. Fox is to notify Mr. Cooper as to what she needs and the Parties are to cooperate. Ms. Fox stated that they will start with an informal accounting. Ms. Conner is to provide an informal accounting by July 31, 2020. The Petition to Determine Those Entitled to Distribution of Estate, and the Petition to Determine Distribution Rights are continued to Friday July 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties agree that there will be no witnesses and no discovery.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate.
Off Calendar Oct-16-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-07-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Armon Cooper for and with Barbara Conner.
John Rueppel and Vincent Yu for Jacob Dellinger (individually/Guardian Ad Litem for Jillian), Jillian Dellinger (Minor) and Clare Kottwitz (Objectors).
Deborah Fox for and with Debra Dolch

In response to the Court's inquiry Ms. Fox and Mr. Rueppel indicated that they are open to mediation, but Mr. Cooper indicated that he is not. Discussion was held regarding the conditional disclaimers, the effect of the disclaimers, the disclaimers being done after Ms. Conner was removed as the Administrator, Ms. Conner paying $150,000.00 out of her personal money outside of the estate, Ms. Conner having no power to enter into any settlement, whether or not Mr. Havassy's declaration regarding the lost will should be considered as evidence with this being a contested matter now that he has passed away and would not be able to testify, why Ms. Connor filed the first petition, etc. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to whether or not Jacob and Jillian have standing to nominate Debra Dolch, whether or not the lost will should be admitted into probate, whether the disclaimers of Richard and Michael are a settlement, whether Barbara has priority, etc. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court noted that there is nothing before it with respect to approval of a settlement and stated that its preference is to appoint Ms. Dolch. The Court granted Ms. Dolch's Petition for Letters of Administration with limited authority (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $80,000.00 is required. Status on close of estate is set for January 25, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Rueppel is to prepare a form of order that denies Ms. Conner's petition.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Apr-17-2019 Continued To Jun-12-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Armon Cooper for Barbara Conner (Petitioner).
Jacob Dellinger appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Mr. Dellinger informed the Court that Jillian Dellinger and Claire Kottwitz are with him. Mr. Dellinger requested a 45-day continuance to file his objections, stated that he is not familiar with the probate process, but he intends to file something, and that he has an Attorney that was recommended to him, but he has not contacted him. Mr. Cooper stated that this is the first he has heard from anyone, that he sent notice on March 20th and sent earlier notice, that they certainly had time to object or call him, and that he objects to a continuance. Mr. Cooper also stated that this has been delayed several times and that they are ready to go. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to June 12, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects Mr. Dellinger to have an Attorney and to have something filed.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
